senses.definition: plural of quipu, a system of knotted cords used by the Incas for record-keeping and calculation
examples: The archaeologists found several quipus that may reveal Inca census data.
Historically, quipus were used to tally goods and populations.

senses.definition: a genus name in some classifications; historically used in botany or ethnobotany to refer to certain plants; note: usage is rare and context-specific
examples: Refer to the local flora for any plant labeled quira.
Quira appears in some old botanical texts.

senses.definition: of or relating to a quire (a set of manuscript leaves) or quirk; rare, specialized usage
examples: The manuscript exhibit displayed a quircal folio dating from medieval times.

senses.definition: a collection of pages; a unit used in bookbinding or printing, typically 24 or 25 sheets; also a choir in some contexts
examples: He bought a quire of parchment for the manuscript.
The choir rehearsed after the service in the quire.

senses.definition: to ask or inquire; to seek information or to complain in a formal way
examples: I quired the librarian about the archive.
He quired why the shipment was delayed.

senses.definition: past tense of quire; to have asked or inquired
examples: She quired the librarian about the rare book.
He quired the status of the order yesterday.

senses.definition: plural of quire; a set of sheets of paper or a grouping in manuscript context
examples: Several quires of parchment were stored in the chest.
The printer needed to order additional quires for the project.

senses.definition: in relation to or according to a quire or set of sheets; used in rare bibliographic contexts
examples: The binding was described quirewise in the catalog.

senses.definition: relating to the Quirinal Hill or the Roman Quirinal palace; used in historical or classical contexts
examples: The Quirinal Palace overlooks Rome's city center.

senses.definition: items or objects associated with the Quirinal (rare or historical) or Latin-era trivia; used in scholarly contexts
examples: The museum's quirinalia collection included coins and manuscripts.

senses.definition: a small decorative item or ornament; term found in some dialects or historical texts
examples: The cabinet was carved with a tiny quirinca.

senses.definition: present participle of quire; to inquire or ask
examples: She is quiring about the documents.
He is quiring after the status of the shipment.

senses.definition: a singer in a choir, especially in medieval or religious contexts; a member of a quire (choir) in church
examples: The quirister led the chant during the liturgy.

senses.definition: plural of quirister; singers in a choir, especially in historical or religious settings
examples: The quiristers stood ready to perform the hymn.

senses.definition: a person with a quirky or unusual personality; used humorously
examples: He's a quirky person—an obvious quiritarian at heart.

senses.definition: relating to quiritarian; describing quirky or odd traits
examples: Her quiritarian charm made the crowd smile.

senses.definition: a member of the Roman people; in some contexts a citizen or a person of origin in ancient Rome
examples: The quirite citizen participated in the senate meeting.

senses.definition: plural of quirite; multiple Roman citizens or people of Romann origin in historical texts
examples: The quirites gathered to discuss the law.

senses.definition: a small, unusual habit or feature that makes someone or something different
examples: Her morning ritual includes drinking coffee before any work.
That quirky little quirk makes the character memorable.

senses.definition: to produce a strange or peculiar motion or behavior; to work in an unusual way
examples: The machine quirks when overloaded.
The device quirks and then shuts down.

senses.definition: past tense of quirk; produced a peculiar motion or behavior
examples: The lever quirked to the left and then stopped.
The system quirked once and resumed normal operation.

senses.definition: more peculiar or idiosyncratic than usual
examples: Her taste in fashion is quirkier than most people’s.
The plot became quirkier as the book progressed.

senses.definition: most peculiar or idiosyncratic
examples: That is the quirkiest idea I have ever heard.
Her quirkiest outfit won the fashion show.

senses.definition: in a quirky or unusual way
examples: The animation ends quirkily, with an unexpected twist.
He spoke quirkily, making the crowd smile.

senses.definition: the quality of being quirky or unusual
examples: Her quirkiness makes her stand out in a crowd.
The film's quirkiness appealed to indie audiences.

senses.definition: plural of quirkiness; multiple peculiar traits or features
examples: The quirinesses of the system kept surprising users.

senses.definition: present participle of quirk; performing a quirky action or motion
examples: The gadget is quirking as it processes data.
People were quirking their eyebrows in disbelief.
